title: uuf2bin
text: uuf2bin Return a pointer to the binary data in a basic parameter or entity field. Explain this code long uuf2bin (UHACT hOpr, int seqNr, UBYTE** pData, long* sData); Copy Parameters hOpr The handle to the operation or entity parameter seqNr The sequence number of the basic parameter or entity field (sequence numbers start with 1) pData (Output) A pointer to the binary data sData (Output) The length of the binary data Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description uuf2bin returns a pointer to the binary value of a basic parameter or entity field. It also returns the length of the data in the variable pointed to by Data. You would typically use this function to get data from a parameter or field of type raw or image . The allocated data must be freed after use, by calling unifree . uuf2bin converts the data from Uniface internal format to external format. If you need to access the data in Uniface internal format, use uprmgets rather than uuf2bin . Example: 3GL copy functions The following examples demonstrate the use of the functions ubin2uf , udbl2uf , ulong2uf , uuf2bin , uuf2dbl , and uuf2long . The service TESTSVC contains two operations: GETP which has three output parameters of types numeric , float , and raw , and SETP which has three input parameters of types numeric , float , and raw . These two services are defined as follows: Explain this code operation getp params numeric pnum : out; float pflt : out; raw praw : out; endparams pnum = 147483648 pflt = 3.1415926535897 fileload/raw "d:\temp\db2cli.gif", praw end operation setp params numeric pnum : in; float pflt : in; raw praw : in; endparams putmess "pnum = %%pnum" putmess "pflt = %%pflt" putmess "praw = %%praw" end Copy The following 3GL code will activate GETP, retrieve the three values, change them a bit, then pass them to SETP, which prints them to the message frame. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> test_params () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operation handles */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UBYTE* pBin; /* Pointer to binary parameter data */ long binLen; long longVal; double dblVal; ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); /* Instantiate TESTSVC */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETP", 0, &Op1);/* Instantiate operation GETP*/ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"SETP", 0, &Op2);/* Instantiate operation SETP*/ uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETP */ uuf2long(hOp1, 1, &longVal); /* Get long value */ uuf2dbl (hOp1, 2, &dblVal); /* Get double value */ uuf2bin (hOp1, 3, &pBin, &binLen); /* Get pointer to binary data*/ ulong2uf(hOp2, 1, longVal / 2); /* Put long value */ udbl2uf (hOp2, 2, dblVal / 2); /* Put double value */ ubin2uf (hOp2, 3, pBin, 20); /* Put 20 bytes of binary data*/ uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate operation GETP */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ unifree (hOp1, pBin); /* Free the binary data */ ufreeh (&Op1); /* Free GETP operation handle*/ ufreeh (&Op2); /* Free SETP operation handle*/ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy Here is what is SETP prints to the message frame: Explain this code pnum = 73741824 pflt = 1.57079632679485e+000 praw = GIF89a|R{$q{#|i{!{!{!{!{!{F{!{!{! Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. ubin2uf Copy binary data to a basic parameter or entity field.
